topic: Aetheric Refraction §7.11 — EXP-539 re-application verification + §7.11 k fix (q439)
change_summary: Decider commit c905aa1 re-applied EXP-539 patches (reverted by fe94fb8 stale-base push) AND added a new §7.11 k=0.097→0.18 fix patch (closes c2 h2 §7.11 instance). Verification of HEAD (3c5fc72): patches 1 (§1.5 k), 3 (§7.11 WIN equivalence), and the new §7.11 k fix landed correctly. Patch 2 (dispersion caveat) landed in §1.5 (part1), NOT in §7.11 (part7) — the §7.11 dispersion paragraph remains untouched. c2 hole 1 (regression) ADDRESSED. c2 h2 (~0.097 inconsistency) ADDRESSED in both sections. c2 h3 (§7.11 dispersion caveat) STILL OPEN — patch landed in wrong section. c2 h4 (§7.11 WIN equivalence) ADDRESSED.
our_argument_summary: Argument unchanged from c1/c2 (n(r) at 1.44/2.84/21.53 must produce inseparable optical signatures; absent signatures absorbed by re-tuning 0.20; absorbability proves unfalsifiability). The four patches re-applied this cycle do not change the structural argument; they tighten arithmetic consistency (§1.5/§7.11 k=0.18) and add the WIN-017/-026 shared-property clause. The §7.11 dispersion caveat that was supposed to close c2 h3 was applied to §1.5 instead.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No new code analysis since c2. §7.11 critiques a mechanism (n(r)) not a single computed value.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 0.20 coefficient free; H(r) functional form adjustable; V14 always available — unchanged.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — section makes no code-level claims.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Falsification-framing section, not tied to a specific monitor.py WIN. Unchanged from c1/c2.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Aetheric refraction is dome-specific construct; standard atmospheric refraction acknowledged in kernel-of-truth is a different mechanism. Unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 0.20 coefficient was fitted to V13 data; the absorbability proof shows it is re-tuned whenever new observations disagree. Unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: n(r) is derived from H(r) — but the derivation is parametric (0.20 is a free fit) not first-principles. Unchanged.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER from c2 h3 (and originally c1 h3) — STILL OPEN. Decider commit c905aa1 claims 'EXP-539 p2: dispersion wavelength-dependence caveat (ISS-2400, corrected find pattern)' was re-applied. Verification of HEAD sections.json shows the dispersion caveat ('note: this signature requires wavelength-dependent n — the dome has not committed to this, making dispersion a conditional rather than necessary prediction; the angular-position shift and total-internal-reflection signatures below hold regardless of wavelength-dependence and are the stronger falsifiers') was added to §1.5 (part1, idx 35847 — modifying the 'appear as sharp points, identical to northern stars' sentence). The §7.11 (part7) dispersion paragraph at part7 idx 37541 — which is the paragraph c2 h3 explicitly flagged — is UNCHANGED: 'Stars near the southern horizon resolve as sharp points — no rainbow smearing despite a predicted n of 2.84 (for context, diamond at n = 2.42 produces the visible "fire" of cut gemstones).' has no wavelength-dependence qualifier. ISS-2400 was titled 'SEC-7.11 c1 H3: chromatic dispersion falsification framing needs n(r) wavelength caveat' and its location field reads 'data/sections.json: SEC-7.11 test-executed dispersion paragraph' — but the patch landed in SEC-1.5 instead. ISS-2400 is marked 'fixed by EXP-539-patch' in closed-issues.json, but the actual §7.11 paragraph still has the unqualified dispersion claim. A dome defender can still quote §7.11 verbatim and answer 'wavelength-dependent n is not part of the dome\'s posit; aetheric refraction may be monochromatic; your dispersion falsifier does not apply.' The §1.5 caveat is valuable on its own (and arguably the more visible place for general readers), but it doesn\'t close ISS-2400\'s specific target.
holes_found.recommendation: Two options. (a) Apply an additional patch to §7.11 with find_pattern 'no rainbow smearing despite a predicted n of 2.84 (for context, diamond at n = 2.42 produces the visible "fire" of cut gemstones).' and replace_pattern 'no rainbow smearing despite a predicted n of 2.84 (for context, diamond at n = 2.42 produces the visible "fire" of cut gemstones; this signature requires wavelength-dependent n, which the dome has not committed to — see §1.5 for the conditional-prediction caveat).' This both closes the §7.11 hole and back-references the §1.5 treatment to avoid duplicating the full caveat. (b) Re-open ISS-2400 (or open a new ISS) referencing 'SEC-7.11 test-executed dispersion paragraph still lacks wavelength-dependence caveat — EXP-539 p2 landed in §1.5 only'. The decider can then either route to a new EXP or self-apply option (a).

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: ISS-2400 marked 'fixed by EXP-539-patch' in closed-issues.json but the patch landed in SEC-1.5, not the §7.11 dispersion paragraph the ISS targeted. The §7.11 hole remains. Recommended: re-open ISS-2400 OR open a new ISS referencing the mis-landing, OR self-apply the option-(a) patch above.

stronger_arguments.description: The §1.5 placement IS valuable on its own — the §1.5 paragraph was making a stronger unconditional 'no smearing' claim than §7.11. The caveat there is the more visible win. But for §7.11 audit completeness it would be better to ALSO add the caveat (or a back-reference) to §7.11, because §7.11 is the dedicated unfalsifiability section where a dome defender expects every claim to be hedged.
sources: data/sections.json:part1 idx 35847 (§1.5 dispersion paragraph after c905aa1)

stronger_arguments.description: The mis-landing pattern (find_pattern targets §7.11 phrasing, decider applies to §1.5 with a re-authored find) is itself an audit signal — when the analyst's find_pattern doesn't match HEAD, the decider should NOT silently re-author against a different section; it should defer the patch and surface to operator. Recommend a tinker-level lightweight check: 'if a patch\'s find_pattern doesn\'t substring-match the EXP\'s target_section\'s text, abort the patch and log a self-apply-miss sentinel'.
sources: EXP-539 patch 2 vs c905aa1 self-apply outcome

kernel_of_truth.description: The patches that DID land (data/sections.json:part1 §1.5 k=0.18 fix, part7 §7.11 k=0.18 fix, part7 §7.11 shared-property clause 'invoking a free dome-specific parameter with no globe-model counterpart', and part1 §1.5 dispersion caveat) are all correct. The arithmetic baseline (H(10008) = 8537·exp(-10008/8619) ≈ 2663.7; k = (1.40-1)/(8537/2663.7 - 1) = 0.181) confirms ~0.18 is the right replacement for ~0.097 at the equatorial-ring n=1.40 anchor (data/sections.json:part1 ~0.18 at idx 35726 and part7 ~0.18 at idx 38914). The §7.11 WIN-equivalence clause now reads 'each invoking a free dome-specific parameter with no globe-model counterpart' (data/sections.json:part7 idx ~37000-37500) — that addition tightens the c1 h4 concern.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three of four c2 holes addressed does not equal four of four. The §7.11 dispersion paragraph still treats chromatic dispersion as an automatic prediction (data/sections.json:part7 idx 37541), and ISS-2400 says 'fixed' (monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json:ISS-2400 status='fixed', fixed_by='EXP-539-patch') while the actual paragraph is unchanged. A dome defender can quote §7.11 verbatim and rebut. The fix is small (one find-replace targeting §7.11) but it has to actually happen in §7.11.

deeper_analysis:
VERIFICATION RESULT for q439: Decider c905aa1 ('decider-2026-06-01: EXP-539 patches re-applied (c2 verification), §7.11 k fix, EXP-495/548 integrated, BAU triage 6 items, ISS-2437-2441 filed') re-applied EXP-539 after the fe94fb8 regression AND added a new §7.11-specific k fix. Verification by reading HEAD (3c5fc72) data/sections.json:

(a) c2 hole 1 (MAJOR regression — EXP-539 patches reverted): ADDRESSED. Patch 1's §1.5 effect now present ('requires a coefficient of ~0.18', part1 idx ~35726, found by grep -c 'requires a coefficient of ~0.18' data/sections.json returns 1; the reverted '~0.097' phrasing returns 0). Patch 3's §7.11 WIN-equivalence clause present ('invoking a free dome-specific parameter with no globe-model counterpart', part7 idx ~37000-37500). Build artifact (docs/index.html) carries both ('grep -c "invoking a free dome-specific parameter" docs/index.html' returns 1; 'grep -c "requires a coefficient of ~0.18" docs/index.html' returns 1).

(b) c2 hole 2 (MINOR — cross-section ~0.097 inconsistency in §1.5 and §7.11): ADDRESSED. The §1.5 instance was fixed by EXP-539 patch 1 (verified above). The §7.11 instance was fixed by the new c905aa1 'NEW: §7.11 k=0.097→0.18 fix' patch — verified at part7 idx 38914 'requires ~0.18' replacing 'requires ~0.097'. 'grep -c "requires ~0.097" data/sections.json' returns 0; both wrong-value instances eliminated.

(c) c2 hole 3 (MINOR — §7.11 dispersion caveat carry-over from c1 h3): NOT ADDRESSED. Decider claims 'EXP-539 p2: dispersion wavelength-dependence caveat (ISS-2400, corrected find pattern)'. Verification: 'making dispersion a conditional rather than necessary' (the patch's distinctive substring) appears in part1 only (idx 36023), zero times in part7. Before/after diff: c905aa1^:part1 §1.5 paragraph said 'Stars near the southern horizon appear as sharp points, identical to northern stars.' and c905aa1:part1 §1.5 paragraph now says 'Stars near the southern horizon appear as sharp points, identical to northern stars (note: this signature requires wavelength-dependent n — the dome has not committed to this, making dispersion a conditional rather than necessary prediction; the angular-position shift and total-internal-reflection signatures below hold regardless of wavelength-dependence and are the stronger falsifiers).' MEANWHILE part7 §7.11 dispersion paragraph at idx 37541 is identical in c905aa1^ and c905aa1 ('Stars near the southern horizon resolve as sharp points — no rainbow smearing despite a predicted n of 2.84 (for context, diamond at n = 2.42 produces the visible "fire" of cut gemstones).'). So the caveat was added to §1.5 (helpful, but it's a DIFFERENT paragraph than the one c2 h3 flagged). ISS-2400's 'location' field explicitly reads 'data/sections.json: SEC-7.11 test-executed dispersion paragraph' — the ISS targeted §7.11. Closure ledger says ISS-2400 'fixed by EXP-539-patch' at 2026-05-31T21:13:06Z — that was the original 8f3655b commit before the fe94fb8 revert; closure was not re-validated at c905aa1.

(d) c2 hole 4 (MINOR — §7.11 WIN-017/026 equivalence shared-property carry-over from c1 h4): ADDRESSED. Patch 3 verbatim landed at part7 ~idx 37000-37500. Build artifact verified.

(e) §7.11 k=0.18 verification: re-deriving — H(10008) = 8537 × exp(-10008/8619) = 8537 × 0.31201 = 2663.66; k = (n-1) / (8537/H(r) - 1) = 0.40 / (8537/2663.66 - 1) = 0.40 / (3.205 - 1) = 0.40 / 2.205 = 0.1814. So 0.18 is correct (rounded). Cross-section consistency: both §1.5 and §7.11 now state ~0.18. ✓

(f) Tests: 'node test.js' returns '3797 passed, 0 failed' (one more than the 3796 the c905aa1 commit message claimed — likely a small test added since). No regression test gating EXP-539 patch presence was added in c905aa1 (no test/data/sections-patch-canary.json or similar). c2 recommended this; not done.

Advocate mode: the §1.5 caveat is the more visible fix because §1.5 is in part1 (the document\'s headline argument section). But §7.11 is the dedicated 'unfalsifiability' section where the curmudgeon expects every claim to be conditional-when-conditional. A dome defender reading §7.11 alone — say in a citation from a third party — would still see the unqualified dispersion claim. The mis-landing pattern (find_pattern targets §7.11 phrasing, decider re-authors against a different §1.5 sentence) is also a self-apply pattern worth surfacing: the decider's commit message acknowledged 'corrected find pattern' but didn't surface that the corrected find pattern was IN A DIFFERENT SECTION than the ISS target.

Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-016, -017, -026, -033, -056, -065, -069 verdicts unchanged. The shared-property clause now anchors WIN-017 ↔ WIN-026 equivalence more firmly. No cross-WIN drift.

Quantitative verification: all c2 numerical claims confirmed. n(r,k=0.18) and n(r,k=0.20) computations agree with the §7.11 framing; k=0.181 reproduces n=1.40 at equatorial r=10,008 km.

citations_verified: c905aa1 'decider-2026-06-01: EXP-539 patches re-applied (c2 verification), §7.11 k fix, EXP-495/548 integrated' verified by 'git log --oneline | grep c905aa1' and 'git show c905aa1 --stat'
Patch 1 (§1.5 k=0.18) landed: 'grep -c "requires a coefficient of ~0.18" data/sections.json' returns 1; 'grep -c "requires a coefficient of ~0.097" data/sections.json' returns 0
Patch 3 (§7.11 shared-property clause) landed at part7 idx ~37000-37500: 'invoking a free dome-specific parameter with no globe-model counterpart' present
New §7.11 k fix landed at part7 idx 38914 ('requires ~0.18'); 'requires ~0.097' absent from sections.json
Build artifact docs/index.html: grep counts confirm patches 1, 3, new §7.11 k fix all present

ISS-2400 closed-issues.json record: title 'SEC-7.11 c1 H3: chromatic dispersion falsification framing needs n(r) wavelength caveat', location 'data/sections.json: SEC-7.11 test-executed dispersion paragraph', status 'fixed', fixed_by 'EXP-539-patch'
citations_failed: Decider c905aa1 commit message claim 'EXP-539 p2: dispersion wavelength-dependence caveat (ISS-2400, corrected find pattern)' — caveat landed in §1.5 (part1), NOT in §7.11 (part7); ISS-2400's location field targeted §7.11
citations_unchecked: Whether the 'corrected find pattern' was authored by the decider on the fly vs. retrieved from a separate EXP — would require reading the decider run-trace or comparing EXP-539 file at HEAD vs. at c905aa1 time; recommend tinker investigation
Whether the failure mode (find_pattern targets section A, applied to section B) has occurred on other patches — would require a sweep across recent applied-patches/*.json files

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender reading §7.11 (and only §7.11) can argue: 'You list chromatic dispersion as an automatic prediction of n=2.84 — but n(r) in the dome\'s actual model is not specified as wavelength-dependent. Without wavelength-dependence, a refractive medium produces zero dispersion. Your own §1.5 was patched to acknowledge this; your §7.11 was not. Your falsifier on dispersion does not apply to the dome\'s actual posit.' This defense is tactically valid against §7.11 alone. The fix is a one-sentence addition.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Self-apply a follow-up patch to §7.11 with find_pattern 'no rainbow smearing despite a predicted n of 2.84 (for context, diamond at n = 2.42 produces the visible "fire" of cut gemstones).' and replacement that adds the wavelength-dependent caveat (or back-references §1.5). Add a regression test (e.g., test/data/sections-patch-canary.json) that asserts both §1.5 and §7.11 carry their respective dispersion caveats — c2 recommended this for the EXP-539 set; not added in c905aa1.

cross_win_consistency.notes: All seven cited WINs have verdicts consistent with §7.11 framing. The new shared-property clause ('invoking a free dome-specific parameter with no globe-model counterpart') makes the WIN-017 ↔ WIN-026 equivalence claim structural rather than asserted. No cross-WIN drift detected by the patches.

claims_checked: 'requires a coefficient of ~0.18' count in data/sections.json: 1 (§1.5 part1) ✓
'requires ~0.18' count in data/sections.json: 1 (§7.11 part7 — new fix) ✓
'wavelength-dependent n' count: 1 (in part1 §1.5, not part7) — landed in WRONG section per ISS-2400
'making dispersion a conditional rather than necessary' count: 1 (part1 §1.5 only; absent from part7) — confirms patch 2 mis-landing
quantitative_verification.computations: H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619); n(r,k) = 1 + k·(8537/H(r) − 1). At r=10,008 km equatorial radius: H=2663.66, n=1.40 → k=0.181 ≈ 0.18. At r=20,015 km disc edge: H=8537·exp(-20015/8619)=8537·0.0972=830.2, n=1+k(8537/830.2 − 1) = 1 + k·9.28. With k=0.20: n=2.86 ≈ 2.84 (dome's claimed disc-edge value). With k=0.27: n=3.51 ≈ 3.49 (older Height-Table value). Consistent with §7.11's multi-version-zoo framing.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Patch 2 (dispersion caveat) landed in §1.5 not §7.11 — ISS-2400's target location was §7.11. §7.11 dispersion paragraph remains unchanged. ISS-2400 prematurely closed.

summary_for_decider:
VERIFICATION RESULT (q439): Of 4 patches the decider committed in c905aa1, 3 landed correctly and closed their target c2 holes (h1 EXP-539 patches re-applied; h2 ~0.097 fixed in both §1.5 and §7.11; h4 §7.11 WIN-equivalence clause). One patch (EXP-539 p2 'dispersion caveat') landed in §1.5 (part1) but NOT in §7.11 (part7). c2 hole 3 (the §7.11 carry-over from c1 h3) is STILL OPEN. ISS-2400's location field explicitly targeted §7.11; ISS-2400 is marked 'fixed by EXP-539-patch' in closed-issues.json but the §7.11 dispersion paragraph is unchanged.

RECOMMENDED ACTIONS:
1. Either (a) self-apply a §7.11 dispersion-caveat patch (find_pattern 'no rainbow smearing despite a predicted n of 2.84 (for context, diamond at n = 2.42 produces the visible "fire" of cut gemstones).'; replace adds wavelength-dependent caveat or back-refs §1.5) — OR (b) re-open ISS-2400 with a note that the original patch landed in §1.5 and the §7.11 hole remains, and route to a new EXP-NNN.
2. Update closed-issues.json ISS-2400 status to 'partially-fixed' or 're-opened' — keeping 'fixed' is misleading.
3. Add a regression test (e.g., test/data/sections-patch-canary.json) that asserts BOTH §1.5 dispersion caveat AND §7.11 dispersion caveat strings are present in data/sections.json. c2 recommended this for the broader EXP-539 patch set; c905aa1 didn't add it.
4. Consider a tinker-level proposal: 'self-apply guard — abort the patch (and log a sentinel) if a patch\'s find_pattern does not substring-match the section_key the EXP declared as target_section'. The §1.5 vs §7.11 mis-landing in c905aa1 would have been caught by such a guard (EXP-539 p2's intended section_key was §7.11; the matching substring 'wavelength-dependent n' replacement landed in part1).

Verdict: minor_edit. The §7.11 unfalsifiability argument is unaffected; the polish is one paragraph short of full c2 closure. PROP-009 queue_id=439 set on this review file.

PRELUDE: dome PAT scope verified prior to clone (HTTP 200, prefix github_pat_11AQFYC...).

Batched: this c3 is batch_position 1 of 2; q440 SEC-part6 verification follows as batch_position 2 (SEC-part6-EXP495-548-verify.c1.json). q441 SEC-part6-6.14-heterogeneity deferred to next run as singleton — the EXP-544 P2 addition is ~430 words of new prose with new arithmetic claims (-10.7/-28.9/-8.4/-21.7 nT magnetic predictions, -10.9×1.672 multiplier verification) that need dedicated curmudgeon attention rather than batch contamination.
